overallIndex.note: 34% above national average; driven primarily by housing costs

medianHouseholdIncome.note: Average household income reported at $111,241 by another source

brackets.description: Top bracket (one of highest in US)

stateIncomeTaxRate.note: Oregon has a 'kicker' refund when revenues exceed forecasts (9.863% of prior year tax liability for 2025 returns)

salesTaxRate.note: Oregon is one of five states with NO sales tax. This is a significant advantage over most states.

propertyTaxRate.note: Deschutes County effective rate varies by area (Redmond highest at 0.72%, Terrebonne lowest at 0.51%). Oregon's Measure 50 limits assessed value growth to 3% per year, so assessed value is often much lower than market value.

estimatedTaxOn400kHome.note: Estimated based on 0.59%-0.72% effective rate. However, Oregon taxes assessed value (not market value), which is typically much lower. Actual tax may be significantly less on a home purchased at $400K market value if assessed value is lower.

unemploymentRate.trend: Rising — up 0.6 percentage points from a year prior
unemploymentRate.note: Uses Deschutes County data; Sisters-specific data not available

costOfLiving.topEmployersNote: Uses Deschutes County / Central Oregon data. Sisters itself has limited major employers; most residents commute to Bend or work remotely.

medianHomePrice.note: Prices vary significantly: Redfin reports $690K median (Nov 2025), Movoto reported $797K (Oct 2025). DataUSA shows $631K median property value (2024). Market is softening.

marketConditions.trend: Market is cooling. Longer time on market, fewer sales, declining prices year-over-year.

ruralLandPricing.note: Average based on 639 acres of listed land. Individual parcels vary enormously by location, irrigation, and improvements.

keyAreas.name: Cascade Meadow Ranch
keyAreas.description: Premier equestrian community in Sisters. 360-acre gated ranch, 24 lots (1.63-2.96 acres), surrounded by USDA Forest Service lands. Indoor/outdoor arenas, full-care horse staff, 100 acres of turnout pastures, tack room, direct trail access to Deschutes National Forest.
keyAreas.note: Small lot sizes but excellent equestrian amenities. Not suitable for 10+ acre ranch.

keyAreas.name: Tumalo Area
keyAreas.description: Between Bend, Redmond, and Sisters. Known for small farms and horse properties. Larger acreage available (5-35+ acres). Irrigated pastures via Tumalo Irrigation District. Some properties back to BLM land.
keyAreas.note: Best option for 10+ acre horse/ranch property close to Sisters

keyAreas.name: Camp Sherman / Metolius River Area
keyAreas.description: Rural area northwest of Sisters near the Metolius River. More remote, heavily forested. Limited ranch properties but scenic rural living.

keyAreas.name: Tollgate
keyAreas.description: Family-friendly housing community 2 miles west of Sisters on Hwy 20. Seasonal pool, tennis, basketball. More residential than ranch.

keyAreas.name: Crossroads
keyAreas.description: Tucked in the ponderosas near Sisters. Residential neighborhood with forested lots.

keyAreas.name: Black Butte Ranch
keyAreas.description: Resort community 9 miles NW of Sisters. 1,800 acres. Golf, horseback riding, swimming. Mix of full-time residents and vacation homes. Not ranch/acreage lifestyle.

horsePropertyAvailability.zoningNote: Oregon land use law no longer allows new forest and sagebrush subdivisions, making existing rural lots/neighborhoods special and somewhat limited. Deschutes County has Exclusive Farm Use (EFU) and Forest (F1/F2) zoning that allows agricultural/equestrian use on larger parcels. Rural residential zones exist but new subdivision of rural land is restricted.
horsePropertyAvailability.source: Cascade Meadow Ranch HOA / Fay Ranches / Central Oregon Realty Group

notableForFamilies: Tollgate — family-friendly community with pool, 2 mi from Sisters
In-town Sisters — walkable to schools and shops
Tumalo area — best for ranch/horse families wanting acreage
Cascade Meadow Ranch — premier equestrian, smaller lots

propertyTaxOn400kHome.note: Based on effective rate of 0.59%-0.72%. Oregon's Measure 50 means assessed value grows only 3%/year and is typically well below market value, so actual taxes on a newly purchased $400K home would depend on the assessed value at the time of purchase.

inSisters.note: Sisters Christian Academy (classical model) permanently CLOSED as of June 2020. No classical Christian school currently operates in Sisters proper.

withinDrivingDistance.name: Morning Star Christian School
withinDrivingDistance.approach: Christ-centered with STEM focus (Project Lead the Way). NOT classical model.

withinDrivingDistance.name: Three Sisters Adventist Christian School
withinDrivingDistance.note: Oldest Christian school in Central Oregon, since 1924.

accsSchoolsInOregon.note: No ACCS (Association of Classical Christian Schools) member schools exist in the Bend/Sisters/Central Oregon area. Nearest ACCS schools are in the Portland metro (St. Stephen's Academy, Veritas School) — 3+ hours away.

classicalChristianSchools.gap: There is a SIGNIFICANT GAP in classical Christian education in the Sisters/Bend area. No classical Christian day school currently operates within 30 minutes. The closest options are homeschool co-ops or online programs like Logos Online School.

otherPrivateSchools.name: Seven Peaks School
otherPrivateSchools.note: Top-rated private school in Bend, non-religious

otherPrivateSchools.name: St. Francis of Assisi Catholic School

classicalConversations.note: Classical Conversations has communities in all 50 states and Oregon. The CC community search tool requires interactive use to find specific Bend/Sisters communities. Oregon has active CC communities statewide. Prospective families should search at classicalconversations.com/community-search/ or contact CC directly.

otherCoOps.name: Cascade Christian Co-op
otherCoOps.description: Homeschool group supporting Central Oregon families through supplementary classes, social events, field trips, and prayer.

otherCoOps.name: Commonplace Sisters
otherCoOps.description: Classes, private lessons in art, music, drama, and education for Central Oregon community.

stateSupport.organization: OCEANetwork (Oregon Christian Home Education Association Network)
stateSupport.description: Supports, encourages, protects and advances private Christian home education in Oregon. Maintains directory of affiliated support groups.

inSisters.note: No PCA, OPC, EPC, or CREC congregation currently exists in Sisters proper. Sisters has approximately 10 churches, none Reformed/Presbyterian in the confessional sense.

inBendArea.name: Grace Reformed Presbyterian Church
inBendArea.services: Education Hour: Sunday 9:00 AM, Lord's Service: Sunday 10:30 AM
inBendArea.theology: Committed to Biblical and historic Christian faith in the Reformed tradition. Psalm singing.

pcaPresence.note: NO PCA (Presbyterian Church in America) church exists in Bend, Redmond, Sisters, or anywhere in Central Oregon. The nearest PCA churches are in the Willamette Valley (Newberg, Salem, Portland area — 2.5-3+ hours away). The Pacific Northwest Presbytery lists zero Central Oregon congregations.

epcPresence.note: No EPC (Evangelical Presbyterian Church) congregation found in Bend/Sisters/Central Oregon. An ECO church plant (Crux) was being established in Bend by Rev. Pete Santucci, but current status is unclear.
epcPresence.source: EPC Presbytery of the Pacific Northwest

crecPresence.note: No CREC (Communion of Reformed Evangelical Churches) congregation found in Bend/Sisters/Central Oregon. Nearest CREC churches appear to be in Portland metro area.

reformedChurches.summary: The Reformed church landscape in Sisters/Central Oregon is THIN. Only one OPC church (Grace Reformed Presbyterian) and one Reformed Baptist church (Sovereign Grace) exist in Bend, both ~25-30 minutes from Sisters. No PCA, EPC, or CREC congregations exist in the area. This is a significant consideration for families prioritizing Reformed community.

churchesInSistersProper.name: Sisters Community Church
churchesInSistersProper.ministries: Marriage ministry, youth ministry, men's/women's groups, life groups, community care team, Wellhouse Market food pantry (serves 80 families/week)
churchesInSistersProper.note: Largest and most active church in Sisters. Contemporary and traditional worship. Strong community presence.

churchesInSistersProper.name: Wellhouse Church
churchesInSistersProper.denomination: Non-denominational (formerly Westside Sisters, a satellite of Westside Church Bend)
churchesInSistersProper.note: Became independent church in 2020. Runs Wellhouse Market food pantry.

churchesInSistersProper.name: Episcopal Church of The Transfiguration
churchesInSistersProper.address: 121 North Brooks Camp Rd, Sisters, OR

churchesInSistersProper.name: Shepherd of the Hills Lutheran Church
The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

christianCommunityOrganizations: Wellhouse Market — food pantry serving 80 families/week (run by Wellhouse Church)
Sisters Community Church Community Care Team — benevolence ministry
Bend Ministerial Association — ecumenical clergy group in Bend

summer.note: Warm and dry. Cool nights. Excellent for outdoor activities.

fall.note: Rapid cooling Sep-Nov. Beautiful fall colors.

winter.note: Cold with snow. Below freezing nights.

annualRainfall.note: Semi-arid climate. Much drier than western Oregon.

annualSnowfall.note: Sources vary significantly. BestPlaces reports ~32 inches. Mountains nearby receive much more. Snow season Dec-Apr.

sunnyDaysPerYear.note: Below US average, but significantly sunnier than western Oregon. Central Oregon's 'high desert' climate provides more sun than Cascade west side.

climate.generalDescription: Semi-arid high desert climate at ~3,200 ft elevation. Summers are warm, dry, and sunny. Winters are cold with moderate snowfall. Low humidity year-round. Significantly drier and sunnier than Portland/western Oregon.

highlights: Peterson Ridge Trail System — 25 miles of trails at south edge of town, all ability levels
South Sister Trail — 11.6 miles, 5,000 ft elevation gain, Three Sisters Wilderness
Moraine Lake Trail — 5 miles out-and-back, 1,500 ft gain
Sahalie Falls & Koosah Falls — 2.4 mile loop along McKenzie River
McKenzie Pass Scenic Bikeway — lava flows, Dee Wright Observatory
Pacific Crest Trail access nearby
Three Sisters Wilderness — extensive backcountry hiking
Deschutes National Forest — thousands of miles of trails
hiking.source: Sisters Trails Alliance / AllTrails / Visit Central Oregon

highlights: Cascade Meadow Ranch — indoor/outdoor arenas, 100 acres pasture, full-care staff, forest trail access
Deschutes National Forest trails accessible from multiple trailheads
Black Butte Ranch horseback riding
Sisters Rodeo — one of Oregon's largest, annual since 1940
Tumalo area horse properties with BLM land access
equestrian.note: Strong equestrian culture in Sisters Country. Multiple horse communities and extensive public land trail access.

highlights: Metolius River — world-class fly-fishing paradise (fly-fishing only)
Deschutes River
Suttle Lake — camping and fishing
Multiple alpine lakes in Cascade Range
fishing.localResource: The Fly Fisher's Place — guided trips and gear in Sisters

hoodoo.name: Hoodoo Ski Area
hoodoo.distance: 22 miles west / 25-35 min drive from Sisters
hoodoo.details: 34 runs, freestyle terrain park, cross-country trails. Family-friendly, less crowded. 'Oregon's best ski secret.'

bachelor.name: Mt. Bachelor
bachelor.distance: ~50-60 min drive from Sisters (30 min from Bend)
bachelor.details: Major destination ski resort. 4,300+ acres, 101 runs, 3,365 ft vertical drop. One of the largest ski areas in the Pacific Northwest.

highlights: Peterson Ridge Trail System — hiking and biking
McKenzie Pass Scenic Bikeway
Phil's Trail Complex (near Bend) — world-class mountain biking
Fat biking in winter
Gravel biking, adaptive biking supported

otherActivities: Rock climbing (Smith Rock State Park ~35 min away)

crimeRates.safetyPercentile: 43rd percentile (safer than 43% of all US cities)
crimeRates.comparedToOregon: Lower crime rate than 89% of Oregon communities
crimeRates.safestAreas: North part of city considered safest (1 in 75 chance)
crimeRates.leastSafeAreas: South part of city (1 in 26 chance)

walkabilityAndAmenities.downtownWalkability: Good — downtown Sisters has walkable local shops, restaurants, and galleries along Cascade Ave (Hwy 20)
walkabilityAndAmenities.nearestAirport: Redmond Municipal Airport (RDM) — ~30 min drive. Served by Alaska, American, Delta, United
walkabilityAndAmenities.groceryInSisters: Limited — small grocery stores in town
walkabilityAndAmenities.majorShoppingGrocery: Bend has Costco, Fred Meyer, Trader Joe's, Whole Foods, etc.
walkabilityAndAmenities.medicalAccess: St. Charles Health System in Bend (25-30 min); limited medical facilities in Sisters proper

greaterSistersArea.note: The broader Sisters area including surrounding neighborhoods (Tollgate, Crossroads, Black Butte Ranch, rural areas) is home to 10,000+ people.

populationGrowthTrend.note: After decades of rapid growth, Sisters city population has been declining slightly since 2020. This may reflect pandemic-era reverse migration or measurement methodology. The broader Deschutes County continues to grow.
populationGrowthTrend.source: Oregon Demographics / World Population Review / KTVZ

demographics.educationLevel: 42% with bachelor's degree or higher (vs 36.3% statewide)

communityCharacter.description: Sisters is a vibrant, artsy community with charming 1880s Old West facades and genuine small-town ambience. It's a modern Wild West town with period-appropriate architecture. The town values quality over quantity, connection over distraction, and beauty over efficiency.
communityCharacter.identity: Mix of tourism destination, retirement community, outdoor recreation hub, and creative arts community. NOT primarily a young-family town — median age is 49.6 and nearly 39% are 65+. However, families do live in the broader area and the school district is strong.
communityCharacter.tourism: Significant tourist component — Sisters draws visitors year-round for its western charm, galleries, festivals, and outdoor access. The Sisters Rodeo (since 1940), Sisters Outdoor Quilt Show (largest in the world), and other events are major draws.
festivals: Sisters Rodeo — annual since 1940, one of Oregon's largest
Sisters Outdoor Quilt Show — world's largest outdoor quilt show
Various seasonal events and art walks
communityCharacter.civicEngagement: Active community organizations, strong volunteerism. The Nugget Newspaper serves as community paper. City government is accessible. Small-town feel where 'you'll often see familiar faces around town.'
communityCharacter.source: Travel Oregon / Visit Central Oregon / SistersVacation.com

strengths: Strong public school district (#6 in Oregon)
Family-friendly neighborhoods (Tollgate has pool, tennis, basketball)
Active youth sports and school programs
weaknesses: Older demographic (median age 49.6, 39% over 65) — not a young-family dominant community
Limited amenities — need to drive to Bend for major shopping, medical, dining
High housing costs limit young family entry
Limited job market in Sisters itself

homeschoolCommunity.assessment: Moderate — Central Oregon has homeschool support infrastructure through OCEANetwork and co-ops like Cascade Christian Co-op, but specific Sisters-area homeschool community is likely small. Bend (25 min away) would be the hub for most homeschool activities and co-ops.
resources: Classical Conversations — may have community in Bend area (verify via CC community search)
Cascade Christian Co-op — supplementary classes, social events, field trips
Commonplace Sisters — art, music, drama classes
Logos Online School — online classical Christian education option
